title: The Journey of Learning Web Development
content:
Learning web development is not just about writing code, it is about building something meaningful from scratch. When I first started, everything felt confusing — HTML tags, CSS styling, JavaScript logic — it all seemed overwhelming. But slowly, step by step, things started to make sense.

At the beginning, I focused on understanding the basics. HTML helped me structure the content of a webpage, CSS made it look visually appealing, and JavaScript brought life to the page with interactivity. Even small things like centering a div or handling a button click felt like big achievements.

As I progressed, I moved into backend development using Node.js and Express. This was where things became more interesting. Instead of just designing pages, I could now handle data, create routes, and build full applications. I learned how requests and responses work, how servers communicate with browsers, and how data flows in an application.

One of the most exciting parts was building a blog application. It allowed me to create, read, update, and delete posts. I also learned how to store data in JSON files and dynamically display it on the webpage. Debugging errors became a regular part of my journey, but each error taught me something new.

There were moments of frustration when things didn’t work as expected. Sometimes the problem was a small typo, other times it was a logic issue. But solving those problems gave me confidence and improved my understanding. I realized that coding is not about knowing everything, but about learning how to solve problems.

Now, I see web development as a continuous journey. There is always something new to learn — better design, cleaner code, improved performance, and new technologies. The more I practice, the more comfortable I become.

In the end, web development is not just a skill, it is a mindset. It teaches patience, problem-solving, and creativity. And the best part is seeing your ideas come to life on the screen.
author: Tajul

title: Building a Blog App
content: Today I created a blog app where I can add, edit, and delete posts. It uses Express and stores data in a JSON file.
author: Rahul

title: Web Development Journey
content: I started learning web development recently. HTML, CSS, and JavaScript are the core technologies. Now I am exploring backend.
